tasks.title: Repository cleanup: remove build/ and duplicate files
tasks.description: Remove the committed build/ artifact directory and the macOS ' 2' duplicate files, and add a guard so they cannot reappear.
tasks.details: Delete build/ (already in .gitignore) and the duplicates: src/avera/traceability/index 2.py and reports/fixtures/bms-mixed-results/avera-{report 2.md, report 2.json, evidence-graph 2.json}. Copies are archived on T7 (/Volumes/T7Projects/AVERA_archive/duplicates_2026-06-27). Add a CI check or pre-commit hook that fails if any '* 2.*' file or build/ is tracked.
tasks.testStrategy: git ls-files | grep -E ' 2\.|^build/' returns nothing; CI guard fails on a deliberately added '* 2.*' file.

tasks.title: Consolidate the REST API (avera_api canonical)
tasks.description: Remove or formally retire the legacy src/avera/api/ package; avera_api is the single canonical REST API.
tasks.details: avera_api (src/avera_api/main.py) is the pyproject 'avera-api' entry point, documented in README, and imported by tests/test_api.py and tests/test_api_evidence_pack.py. src/avera/api/ is referenced by nothing except itself. It is now marked DEPRECATED (docstring + runtime DeprecationWarning in create_app). Remaining: after a green pytest run, delete src/avera/api/ (git rm -r src/avera/api) and confirm nothing imports it.
tasks.testStrategy: grep -rn 'avera.api' src tests shows no real imports; full pytest suite green after removal.

subtasks.title: Mark avera.api deprecated (done)
subtasks.description: Docstrings + DeprecationWarning added, pointing to avera_api.

subtasks.title: Remove src/avera/api/ after green tests
subtasks.description: git rm -r src/avera/api once the suite is confirmed green.

tasks.title: Agent-OS consistency guard
tasks.description: Add a check that every file path referenced by the 00_AGENT_MENU docs actually exists.
tasks.details: The agent operating system (AGENTS.md, 00_AGENT_MENU/ROUTER.md, PROJECT_MAP.md, ACTIVE_TASK.md) previously drifted from the codebase (missing root ARCHITECTURE.md, ROUTER pointing at non-existent zone guides, stale module names — now fixed). Add a lightweight test or script that parses those docs for referenced paths and asserts each exists, so drift is caught automatically.
tasks.testStrategy: A pytest test fails when an agent-menu document references a path that does not exist on disk.

tasks.title: AI-verification extension
tasks.description: Gate AI/ML model updates as a first-class change type, with the same evidence/audit guarantees as code changes.
tasks.details: Per ACTIVE_TASK.md and docs/AVERA_AI_EXTENSION.md. Scope: src/avera/adapters/ai_evaluation.py (model eval results -> AVERA verdict), src/avera/ingestion/model_card.py (model metadata loader), fixtures/adas-model-update-regression/ reference scenario, tests/test_ai_model_update_fixture.py. Some of these already exist — reconcile, finish, and document. Invariant: no LLM in the decision path.
tasks.testStrategy: An AI model-update regression fixture is classified deterministically as a regression and produces a complete evidence manifest + audit chain; test_ai_model_update_fixture passes.

tasks.title: Release-readiness hardening in CI
tasks.description: Add packaging build check, ruff lint gate, and coverage to CI.
tasks.details: Add to .github/workflows: a packaging smoke check (python -m build / pip install .) so pyproject readme/entry-points are verified; a ruff lint gate (line-length 100, target py311 already configured); and a coverage measurement step with a published badge. CI should run pytest + ruff + build + coverage on every PR.
tasks.testStrategy: CI workflow runs and passes pytest, ruff, build, and coverage stages on a PR.

tasks.title: Documentation index sync
tasks.description: Ensure docs/AVERA_INDEX.md lists the canonical current documents and flags superseded ones.
tasks.details: docs/ has ~100 files. Update docs/AVERA_INDEX.md so a reader (or investor) has one entry point: list the canonical, current documents, mark superseded ones, and verify all links resolve.
tasks.testStrategy: Every link in docs/AVERA_INDEX.md resolves to an existing file; manual review confirms superseded docs are flagged.
